Headline: Sulbactam
Description: Sulbactam is a β-lactamase inhibitor. This drug is given in combination with β-lactam antibiotics to inhibit β-lactamase, an enzyme produced by bacteria that destroys the antibiotics.[1]

Headline: Medina County University Center
Description: The Medina County University Center, or "MCUC," is an extension of the University of Akron.  A three-story structure, this facility opened in 2007 and is located in Lafayette Township in Medina County, Ohio, USA.

Headline: Mont Brulé (Arolla)
Description: Mont Brulé (also known as Mont Brûlé or Mont Braoulé) is a mountain of the Pennine Alps, located on the Swiss-Italian border, east of the Col Collon. On its northern side it overlooks the upper Arolla Glacier.

Headline: Dasht-e Roba
Description: Dasht-e Roba (Persian: دشت ربا, also Romanized as Dasht-e Robā) is a village in Qilab Rural District, Alvar-e Garmsiri District, Andimeshk County, Khuzestan Province, Iran. At the 2006 census, its population was 54, in 9 families.[1]

Headline: USWA World Tag Team Championship
Description: The USWA World Tag Team Championship was the primary professional wrestling tag team championship promoted by the Memphis, Tennessee-based United States Wrestling Association (USWA). The Continental Wrestling Association and World Class Wrestling Association (WCWA) merged in 1989 to form the USWA.[1] In the merger the USWA replaced both the WCWA World Tag Team Championship and the CWA Tag Team Championship with the USWA version.[a][b] The promotion awarded Cactus Jack and Scott Braddock the championship after they won the WCWA championship on August 4, 1989. The USWA closed in 1997, with PG-13 (J. C. Ice and Wolfie D) as the final champions. There were a total of 116 reigns in the eight year lifetime of the championship.[c]
